stem: A circle has center P, and points J and K lie on the circle. Line segments JH and KH are tangent to the circle at points J and K, respectively. If the radius of the circle is 20 millimeters and the perimeter of quadrilateral PJHK is 238 millimeters, what is the distance, in millimeters, between points P and H?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. PJ = PK = 20 (radii), and tangents from an external point are equal: JH = KH. So 2(20) + 2(JH) = 238, giving JH = 99. Since PJ ⊥ JH, by the Pythagorean theorem, PH = √(20² + 99²) = √(400 + 9,801) = √10,201 = 101 millimeters.

Choice B is JH + 20. Choice C gives JH only. Choice D gives the radius.
